risk.desc: Potential salt-air corrosion on exposed fasteners.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coating to all external fasteners.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient cooling air flow through motor pod.
risk.mit: Increase cooling inlet size and optimize airflow path.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during wing spar reinforcement assembly.
risk.mit: Use precision datum features and alignment tools.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access to equipment bay.
risk.mit: Design larger access panels with clear labeling.

risk.desc: Risk of high wind impact on equipment bay door.
risk.mit: Strengthen door hinges and add wind locks.

risk.desc: The spar may not be strong enough for the required loads.
risk.mit: Conduct stress analysis and consider reinforcing the spar.

risk.desc: The anti-rotation key may fail under high rotational loads.
risk.mit: Increase the size or material strength of the anti-rotation key.

risk.desc: The service access panel may not provide sufficient access for maintenance.
risk.mit: Review the design and ensure the service access panel is large enough.

risk.desc: The rib may not provide adequate support for the wing.
risk.mit: Increase the thickness or number of ribs.

risk.desc: The carry-through may not transfer loads effectively.
risk.mit: Review the design and ensure the carry-through is properly sized.

risk.desc: Potential for salt-air corrosion due to offshore wind farm inspection mission.

risk.desc: High-wind launch capability may be compromised if fasteners are not adequately secured.

risk.desc: Lightening pockets may weaken the structure if not properly designed.
risk.mit: Optimize lightening pocket design for structural integrity.

risk.mit: Include thermal expansion allowances in the design.

risk.desc: Control surface actuation may be impeded by hinge line clearance issues.
risk.mit: Ensure adequate clearance for hinge line actuation.

risk.desc: The subsystem may not be robust enough for high-wind launches due to its material choice and design.
risk.mit: Consider reinforcing the design with additional ribs or using a more robust material like carbon fiber.

risk.desc: The mass budget is tight, and any additional components may exceed the allowed weight.
risk.mit: Optimize the design to reduce weight without compromising structural integrity.

risk.desc: The design may not be fully compatible with the existing wing structure due to minor interface discrepancies.
risk.mit: Review and adjust the interface dimensions to ensure a perfect fit.

risk.desc: The control horn mounting may experience excessive wear over time due to the chosen fastening method.
risk.mit: Consider using a different fastening method or adding a wear-resistant material around the mounting point.

risk.desc: The service access cover may not provide sufficient protection against salt-air corrosion.
risk.mit: Apply a corrosion-resistant coating or use a more durable material for the cover.
